CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), age INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, age) VALUES (1, 'Alice', 30);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, age) VALUES (2, 'Bob', 25);
UPDATE test_table SET age = 31 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Charlie', age = 29 WHERE id = 2;
WITH age_increment AS (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE age < 30) UPDATE test_table SET age = age + 2 WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM age_increment);
UPDATE test_table AS t SET name = (SELECT name FROM test_table WHERE id = 2) WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Eve' WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE age >= 30);
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Test' WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE age < 25);
UPDATE test_table SET age = (SELECT MAX(age) FROM test_table) WHERE name = 'Alice';
UPDATE test_table AS t1 SET age = (SELECT AVG(age) FROM test_table) WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM test_table AS t2 WHERE t1.id = t2.id);
UPDATE test_table SET age = CASE WHEN age < 25 THEN age + 5 ELSE age END;
DELETE FROM test_table WHERE id = (UPDATE test_table SET age = age - 1 WHERE id = 1 RETURNING id);
